specific_mechanism: Thigmomorphogenesis: repeated mechanical stimulation (wind, touch) elicits a calcium-mediated mechanosensing cascade, ZFP/jasmonate signaling, and downstream growth-plan revision; the plant shortens stems, thickens diameters, and reinforces cell walls; the response scales with stimulus frequency × intensity
llm_application: Curriculum design that mimics thigmomorphogenesis: instead of monotonic curricula, expose the trainee LLM to repeated brief mechanical 'perturbations' (controlled adversarial inputs, calibrated noise injection) at calibrated frequency × intensity; the model is rewarded for stem-reinforcement-style robustness (shorter, sturdier capability) rather than for unbounded growth. Frequency × intensity dosing is the lever; the response is structural reinforcement at the level activated by the perturbation.
novelty_claim: Existing adversarial training treats perturbations as a fixed dataset or random schedule. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a calibrated frequency-times-intensity dosing rule that yields stem-reinforcement-style targeted robustness as thigmomorphogenesis does.
